Q1 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following concurrency control protocol ensure both conflict serializability and free from deadlock?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Both time stamp ordering and 2 phase locking protocol ensure conflict serializable but only time stamp ordering protocol provides freedom from deadlock.

Select Snum from Student S where S.Snum NOT IN

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

Not attempted

Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Since the question doesn’t say that the attribute is a prime attribute, hence if the value to some attribute is not known then null can be used time stamp protocol has no deadlock.

SELECT columns
FROM TableA
RIGHT OUTER JOIN TableB
ON A.columnName = B.columnName
WHERE A.columnName IS NULL
returns the following:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(a)

Q7 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are TRUE about an SQL query?
P : An SQL query can contain a HAVING clause even if it does not have a GROUP BY clause.
Q : An SQL query can contain a HAVING clause only if it has a GROUP BY clause.
R : All attributes used in the GROUP BY clause must appear in the SELECT clause.
S : Not all attributes used in the GROUP BY clause need to appear in the SELECT clause.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q8 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are true?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
If disk block allocated for B+ tree index and same size disk block is allocated for B tree index.
Then B tree index access cost is greater than B+ tree index for given distinct keys.
So option (c) is false.

Q10 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a table in a relational database with key field K. A B-tree of order P is used as an access structure on K, where P denotes the maximum, number of tree pointer in a B-tree index node. Assume that K is 12 bytes long; disk block size is 256 bytes; each data pointer PD is 10 bytes along and each block pointer PB is 7 bytes long is order fore each B tree node to fit in a single disk block, the maximum value of P is ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 9
Solution

9

Q12 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider a database system where transactions are executed concurrently. If transaction T1 holds an exclusive lock on resource R1 and requests a shared lock on resource R2, while transaction T2 holds a shared lock on resource R2 and requests an exclusive lock on resource R1, what will happen, transactions executed by using 2PL restriction?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Deadlock occurs when transactions are waiting for locks held by each other, causing a circular dependency that prevents any transaction from progressing.

Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following relational schema:

Assume that, in the suppliers relation above, each supplier and each street within a city has unique name, and (sname, city) forms a candidate key No other functional dependencies are implied other than those implied by primary and candidate keys. Which one of the following is
TRUE about the above schema?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The non-trivial FDs are
1. (sname, city) → street
2. sid → street
3. (sname, city) → sid
4. sid → name
5. sid → city
